root_text.sn55.51:1.1: “Catūhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo.
root_text.sn55.51:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.51:2.3: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.51:2.6: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.51:2.7: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo”ti.
root_text.sn55.51:2.9: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:

translation_text.sn55.51:1.1: “Mendicants, a noble disciple who has four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.51:2.2: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.51:2.6: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.51:2.7: A noble disciple who has these four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
translation_text.sn55.51:2.8: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn55.51:2.9: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn55.51:3.1: “Whoever has faith in the Realized One,
translation_text.sn55.51:3.4: praised and loved by the noble ones;
translation_text.sn55.51:4.1: who has confidence in the Saṅgha,
translation_text.sn55.51:4.4: their life is not in vain.
translation_text.sn55.51:5.1: So let the wise devote themselves
translation_text.sn55.51:5.3: confidence, and insight into the teaching,
translation_text.sn55.51:5.4: remembering the instructions of the Buddhas.”
